title: clematis lasiantha subsp. pauciflora
intro: Clematis lasiantha subsp. pauciflora is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae), native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, and can be used externally to treat wounds and skin irritations.
